controlTree.Caption: Sales Order
controlTree.AboutText: Choose the order details and fill in order lines with quantities of what you are selling. Post the order when you are ready to ship or invoice. This creates posted sales shipments and posted sales invoices.

Children.Caption: View
Children.Synopsis: Open the page in read-only mode

Children.Caption: Edit
Children.Synopsis: Make changes on the page

Children.Caption: New
Children.Synopsis: Create a new entry.

Children.Caption: Delete
Children.Synopsis: Delete the information

Children.Caption: Refresh
Children.Synopsis: Update the page with any changes made by other users.

Children.Caption: Previous
Children.Synopsis: Go to the previous document of the same type

Children.Caption: Next
Children.Synopsis: Go to the next document of the same type

Children.Caption: Approve
Children.Synopsis: Approve the requested changes.

Children.Caption: Reject
Children.Synopsis: Reject the approval request.

Children.Caption: Delegate
Children.Synopsis: Delegate the approval to a substitute approver.

Children.Caption: Comments
Children.Synopsis: View or add comments for the record.

Children.Caption: Re&lease
Children.Synopsis: Release the document to the next stage of processing. You must reopen the document before you can make changes to it.

Children.Caption: Re&open
Children.Synopsis: Reopen the document to change it after it has been approved. Approved documents have the Released status and must be opened before they can be changed.

Children.Caption: Create Purchase Orders
Children.Synopsis: Create one or more new purchase orders to buy the items that are required by this sales document, minus any quantity that is already available.

Children.Caption: Create Purchase Invoice
Children.Synopsis: Create a new purchase invoice to buy all the items that are required by the sales document, even if some of the items are already available.

Children.Caption: Calculate &Invoice Discount
Children.Synopsis: Calculate the invoice discount that applies to the sales order.

Children.Caption: Get Recurring Sales Lines...
Children.Synopsis: Insert sales document lines that you have set up for the customer as recurring. Recurring sales lines could be for a monthly replenishment order or a fixed freight expense.

Children.Caption: Copy Document...
Children.Synopsis: Copy document lines and header information from another sales document to this document. You can copy a posted sales invoice into a new sales invoice to quickly create a similar document.

Children.Caption: Move Negative Lines...
Children.Synopsis: Prepare to create a replacement sales order in a sales return process.

Children.Caption: Archi&ve Document
Children.Synopsis: Send the document to the archive, for example because it is too soon to delete it. Later, you delete or reprocess the archived document.

Children.Caption: Send IC Sales Order
Children.Synopsis: Send the sales order to the intercompany outbox or directly to the intercompany partner if automatic transaction sending is enabled.

Children.Caption: Reject IC Sales Order
Children.Synopsis: Deletes the order and sends the rejection to the company that created it.

Children.Caption: View Incoming Document
Children.Synopsis: View any incoming document records and file attachments that exist for the entry or document.

Children.Caption: Select Incoming Document
Children.Synopsis: Select an incoming document record and file attachment that you want to link to the entry or document.

Children.Caption: Create Incoming Document from File...
Children.Synopsis: Create an incoming document record by selecting a file to attach, and then link the incoming document record to the entry or document.

Children.Caption: Remove Incoming Document
Children.Synopsis: Remove any incoming document records and file attachments.

Children.Caption: Predict Payment
Children.Synopsis: Predict whether the payment for this sales document will be made on time. Predictions are available only if the Late Payment Prediction extension is enabled.

Children.Caption: Order &Promising
Children.Synopsis: Calculate the shipment and delivery dates based on the item's known and expected availability dates, and then promise the dates to the customer.

Children.Caption: Demand Overview
Children.Synopsis: Get an overview of demand for your items when planning sales, production, projects, or service management and when they will be available.

Children.Caption: Pla&nning
Children.Synopsis: Open a tool for manual supply planning that displays all new demand along with availability information and suggestions for supply. It provides the visibility and tools needed to plan for demand from sales lines and component lines and then create different types of supply orders directly.

Children.Caption: Send A&pproval Request
Children.Synopsis: Request approval of the document.

Children.Caption: Cancel Approval Re&quest
Children.Synopsis: Cancel the approval request.

Children.Caption: Create approval flow
Children.Synopsis: Create a new flow in Power Automate from a list of relevant flow templates.

Children.Caption: Create Inventor&y Put-away/Pick...
Children.Synopsis: Create an inventory put-away or inventory pick to handle items on the document according to a basic warehouse configuration that does not require warehouse receipt or shipment documents.

Children.Caption: Create &Warehouse Shipment
Children.Synopsis: Create a warehouse shipment to start a pick a ship process according to an advanced warehouse configuration.

Children.Caption: P&ost...
Children.Synopsis: Finalize the document or journal by posting the amounts and quantities to the related accounts in your company books.
Children.AboutText: Posting will ship or invoice the quantities on the order, or both. **Post** and **Send** can save the order as a file, print it, or attach it to an email, all in one go.

Children.Caption: Post and New...
Children.Synopsis: Post the sales document and create a new, empty one.

Children.Caption: Post and Send...
Children.Synopsis: Finalize and prepare to send the document according to the customer's sending profile, such as attached to an email. The Send document to window opens first so you can confirm or select a sending profile.

Children.Caption: Test Report...
Children.Synopsis: View a test report so that you can find and correct any errors before you perform the actual posting of the journal or document.

Children.Caption: Remove From Job Queue
Children.Synopsis: Remove the scheduled processing of this record from the job queue.

Children.Caption: Preview Posting
Children.Synopsis: Review the different types of entries that will be created when you post the document or journal.

Children.Caption: Pro Forma Invoice...
Children.Synopsis: View or print the pro forma sales invoice.

Children.Caption: Prepayment &Test Report...
Children.Synopsis: Preview the prepayment transactions that will results from posting the sales document as invoiced.

Children.Caption: Post Prepayment &Invoice...
Children.Synopsis: Post the specified prepayment information.

Children.Caption: Post and Print Prepmt. Invoic&e...
Children.Synopsis: Post the specified prepayment information and print the related report.

Children.Caption: Preview Prepmt. Invoice Posting
Children.Synopsis: Review the different types of entries that will be created when you post the prepayment invoice.

Children.Caption: Post Prepayment &Credit Memo...
Children.Synopsis: Create and post a credit memo for the specified prepayment information.

Children.Caption: Post and Print Prepmt. Cr. Mem&o...
Children.DesignName: Post and Print Prepmt. Cr. Mem&o
Children.Synopsis: Create and post a credit memo for the specified prepayment information and print the related report.

Action.Caption: Post and Print Prepmt. Cr. Mem&o

Children.Caption: Preview Prepmt. Cr. Memo Posting
Children.Synopsis: Review the different types of entries that will be created when you post the prepayment credit memo.

Children.Caption: Preview E-Document Mapping
Children.Synopsis: Preview E-Document Mapping

Children.Caption: Download as PDF
Children.Synopsis: Download the sales order as a PDF file.

Children.Caption: Work Order...
Children.Synopsis: Prepare to registers actual item quantities or time used in connection with the sales order. For example, the document can be used by staff who perform any kind of processing work in connection with the sales order. It can also be exported to Excel if you need to process the sales line data further.

Children.Caption: Pick Instruction
Children.Synopsis: Print a picking list that shows which items to pick and ship for the sales order. If an item is assembled to order, then the report includes rows for the assembly components that must be picked. Use this report as a pick instruction to employees in charge of picking sales items or assembly components for the sales order.

Children.Caption: Email Confirmation...
Children.Synopsis: Send a sales order confirmation by email. The attachment is sent as a .pdf.

Children.Caption: Print Confirmation...
Children.Synopsis: Print a sales order confirmation.

Children.Caption: Attach as PDF...
Children.Synopsis: Create a PDF file and attach it to the document.

Children.Caption: Statistics
Children.Synopsis: View statistical information, such as the value of posted entries, for the record.

Children.Caption: Statistics
Children.Synopsis: View statistical information, such as the value of posted entries, for the record.

Children.Caption: Customer
Children.Synopsis: View or edit detailed information about the customer on the sales document.

Children.Caption: Customer Statistics
Children.Synopsis: View statistical information, such as the value of posted entries, for the sell-to customer on the sales document.

Children.Caption: Dimensions
Children.Synopsis: View or edit dimensions, such as area, project, or department, that you can assign to sales and purchase documents to distribute costs and analyze transaction history.

Children.Caption: Approvals
Children.Synopsis: View a list of the records that are waiting to be approved. For example, you can see who requested the record to be approved, when it was sent, and when it is due to be approved.

Children.Caption: Co&mments
Children.Synopsis: View or add comments for the record.

Children.Caption: Assembly Orders
Children.Synopsis: View ongoing assembly orders related to the sales order.

Children.Caption: Attachments
Children.Synopsis: Add a file as an attachment. You can attach images as well as documents.

Children.Caption: Sales Order
Children.Synopsis: View the selected sales order.

Children.Caption: Synchronize
Children.Synopsis: Send updated data to Dynamics 365 Sales.

Children.Caption: Set Up Coupling
Children.Synopsis: Create or modify the coupling to a Dynamics 365 Sales order.

Children.Caption: Delete Coupling
Children.Synopsis: Delete the coupling to a Dynamics 365 Sales order.

Children.Caption: Synchronization Log
Children.Synopsis: View integration synchronization jobs for the sales order table.

Children.Caption: S&hipments
Children.Synopsis: View related posted sales shipments.

Children.Caption: Invoices
Children.Synopsis: View a list of ongoing sales invoices for the order.

Children.Caption: In&vt. Put-away/Pick Lines
Children.Synopsis: View items that are inbound or outbound on inventory put-away or inventory pick documents for the sales order.

Children.Caption: Warehouse Shipment Lines
Children.Synopsis: View ongoing warehouse shipments for the document, in advanced warehouse configurations.

Children.Caption: Warehouse Pick Lines
Children.Synopsis: View items that are outbound on warehouse pick documents for the sales order.

Children.Caption: Prepa&yment Invoices
Children.Synopsis: View related posted sales invoices that involve a prepayment.

Children.Caption: Prepayment Credi&t Memos
Children.Synopsis: View related posted sales credit memos that involve a prepayment.

Children.Caption: Interaction Log E&ntries
Children.Synopsis: View a list of interaction log entries related to this document.

Children.Caption: Tell me what you want to do. Quickly access actions, pages, reports, documentation, and apps and consulting services.
Dynamics 365 Business Central Help
Children.Synopsis: Get help using Dynamics 365 Business Central.

Children.Caption: No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of the involved entry or record, according to the specified number series.

Children.Caption: Customer No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of the customer who will receive the products and be billed by default.

Children.Caption: Customer Name
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the name of the customer who will receive the products and be billed by default.
Children.AboutText: You can choose existing customers, or add new customers when you create orders. Orders can automatically choose special prices and discounts that you have set for each customer.

Children.Caption: Quote No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of the sales quote that the sales order was created from. You can track the number to sales quote documents that you have printed, saved, or emailed.

Children.Caption: Address
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the address where the customer is located.

Children.Caption: Address 2
Children.Synopsis: Specifies additional address information.

Children.Caption: City
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the city of the customer on the sales document.

Children.Caption: Sell-to County
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the state, province or county of the address.

Children.Caption: Post Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the postal code.

Children.Caption: Country/Region Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the country or region of the address.

Children.Caption: Contact No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of the contact person that the sales document will be sent to.

Children.Caption: Contact Phone No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the telephone number of the contact person at the customer. This is used in the exported electronic document.

Children.Caption: Contact Fax No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the fax number of the contact person at the customer. This is used in the exported electronic document.

Children.Caption: Contact E-Mail
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the email address of the contact person at the customer. This is used in the exported electronic document.

Children.Caption: Contact Role
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the role of the contact person at the customer. This is used in the exported electronic document.

Children.Caption: Phone No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the telephone number of the contact person that the sales document will be sent to.

Children.Caption: Mobile Phone No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the mobile telephone number of the contact person that the sales document will be sent to.

Children.Caption: Email
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the email address of the contact person that the sales document will be sent to.

Children.Caption: Contact
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the name of the person to contact at the customer.

Children.Caption: No. of Archived Versions
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of archived versions for this document.

Children.Caption: Document Date
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the date when the related document was created.

Children.Caption: Posting Date
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the date when the posting of the sales document will be recorded.

Children.Caption: VAT Date
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the date used to include entries on VAT reports in a VAT period. This is either the date that the document was created or posted, depending on your setting on the General Ledger Setup page.

Children.Caption: Order Date
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the date the order was created. The order date is also used to determine the prices and discounts on the document.

Children.Caption: Due Date
Children.Synopsis: Specifies when the related sales invoice must be paid.
Children.AboutText: When you post an order, the invoice gets it's due date. You can set default due dates for orders by assigning payment terms to customers.

Children.Caption: Requested Delivery Date
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the date that the customer has asked for the order to be delivered.

Children.Caption: Promised Delivery Date
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the date that you have promised to deliver the order, as a result of the Order Promising function.

Children.Caption: External Document No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a document number that refers to the customer's or vendor's numbering system.

Children.Caption: Your Reference
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's reference. The content will be printed on sales documents.

Children.Caption: Salesperson Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the name of the salesperson who is assigned to the customer.

Children.Caption: Campaign No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of the campaign that the document is linked to.

Children.Caption: Opportunity No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of the opportunity that the sales quote is assigned to.

Children.Caption: Responsibility Center
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the code of the responsibility center, such as a distribution hub, that is associated with the involved user, company, customer, or vendor.

Children.Caption: Assigned User ID
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the ID of the user who is responsible for the document.

Children.Caption: Job Queue Status
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the status of a job queue entry or task that handles the posting of sales orders.

Children.Caption: Status
Children.Synopsis: Specifies whether the document is open, waiting to be approved, has been invoiced for prepayment, or has been released to the next stage of processing.
Specifies the products or service being offered.

FilterColumns.Caption: Prepmt Amt to Deduct Excl. VAT

Columns.Caption: Type
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the type of transaction that will be posted with the document line. If you select Comment, then you can enter any text in the Description field, such as a message to a customer.

Columns.Caption: No.
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies what you are selling, such as a product or a fixed asset. You’ll see different lists of things to choose from depending on your choice in the Type field.

Columns.Caption: Item Reference No.
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies a reference to the item number as defined by the vendor or customer, or the item's barcode.

Columns.Caption: Description
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies a description of what you’re selling. Based on your choices in the Type and No. fields, the field may show suggested text that you can change it for this document. To add a comment, set the Type field to Comment and write the comment itself here.

Columns.Caption: Location Code
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the inventory location from which the items sold should be picked and where the inventory decrease is registered.

Columns.Caption: Sustainability Account No.
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the value of the Sustainability Account No. field.

Columns.Caption: Quantity
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies how many units are being sold.
Columns.AboutText: The quantity on a line specifies how much of an item a customer is ordering. This quantity determines whether the order qualifies for special prices or discounts.

Columns.Caption: Qty. to Assemble to Order
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies how many units of the sales line quantity that you want to supply by assembly.

Columns.Caption: Reserved Quantity
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies how many units of the item on the line have been reserved.

Columns.Caption: Unit of Measure Code
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies how each unit of the item or resource is measured, such as in pieces or hours. By default, the value in the Base Unit of Measure field on the item or resource card is inserted.

Columns.Caption: Unit Price Excl. VAT
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the price for one unit on the sales line.

Columns.Caption: Line Discount %
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the discount percentage that is granted for the item on the line.

Columns.Caption: Line Amount Excl. VAT
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the net amount, excluding any invoice discount amount, that must be paid for products on the line.

Columns.Caption: Subscription Lines
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the number of Subscription Lines for the sales line.

Columns.Caption: Customer Subscription Contract No.
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the associated Customer Subscription Contract the Subscription Line will be assigned to. If the sales line was created by a Contract Renewal, the Contract No. cannot be edited.

Columns.Caption: Vendor Subscription Contract No.
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the associated Vendor Subscription Contract the Subscription Line will be assigned to. If the sales line was created by a Contract Renewal, the Contract No. cannot be edited.

Columns.Caption: Allocation Account No.
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the allocation account number that will be used to distribute the amounts during the posting process.

Columns.Caption: Qty. to Ship
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the quantity of items that remain to be shipped.
Columns.AboutText: If you want to ship only parts of the order, adjust the *Qty. to Ship* value to that quantity. By common default, the total quantity is shipped.

Columns.Caption: Quantity Shipped
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies how many units of the item on the line have been posted as shipped.

Columns.Caption: Qty. to Invoice
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the quantity that remains to be invoiced. It is calculated as Quantity - Qty. Invoiced.
Columns.AboutTitle: Invoicing more or less than you ship?
Columns.AboutText: Adjust the *Qty. to Invoice* to specify the quantity you want to invoice now. If that is more than you ship, use the prepayment functionality.

Columns.Caption: Quantity Invoiced
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies how many units of the item on the line have been posted as invoiced.

Columns.Caption: Total CO2e
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the value of the Total CO2e field.

Columns.Caption: Total EPR Fee
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the value of the Total EPR Fee field.

Columns.Caption: Prepmt Amt to Deduct Excl. VAT
Qty. to Assign
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies how many units of the item charge are assigned to the line originally.

Columns.Caption: Item Charge Qty. to Handle
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies how many items the item charge will be assigned to on the line. It can be either equal to Qty. to Assign or to zero. If it is zero, the item charge will not be assigned to the line.

Columns.Caption: Qty. Assigned
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the quantity of the item charge that was assigned to a specified item when you posted this sales line.

Columns.Caption: Planned Delivery Date
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the planned date that the shipment will be delivered at the customer's address. If the customer requests a delivery date, the program calculates whether the items will be available for delivery on this date. If the items are available, the planned delivery date will be the same as the requested delivery date. If not, the program calculates the date that the items are available for delivery and enters this date in the Planned Delivery Date field.

Columns.Caption: Planned Shipment Date
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the date that the shipment should ship from the warehouse. If the customer requests a delivery date, the program calculates the planned shipment date by subtracting the shipping time from the requested delivery date. If the customer does not request a delivery date or the requested delivery date cannot be met, the program calculates the content of this field by adding the shipment time to the shipping date.

Columns.Caption: Shipment Date
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies when items on the document are shipped or were shipped. A shipment date is usually calculated from a requested delivery date plus lead time.

Columns.Caption: Afdeling Kode
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 1, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

Columns.Caption: Debitorgruppe Kode
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 2, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

Columns.Caption: Attached Lines Count
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the number of non-inventory product lines attached to the sales line.

Children.Caption: Type
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the type of transaction that will be posted with the document line. If you select Comment, then you can enter any text in the Description field, such as a message to a customer.

Children.Caption: No.

Children.Caption: Item Reference No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a reference to the item number as defined by the vendor or customer, or the item's barcode.

Children.Caption: Description
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a description of what you’re selling. Based on your choices in the Type and No. fields, the field may show suggested text that you can change it for this document. To add a comment, set the Type field to Comment and write the comment itself here.

Children.Synopsis: Specifies the inventory location from which the items sold should be picked and where the inventory decrease is registered.

Children.Caption: Sustainability Account No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the value of the Sustainability Account No. field.

Children.Caption: Quantity
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how many units are being sold.

Children.Caption: Qty. to Assemble to Order
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how many units of the sales line quantity that you want to supply by assembly.

Children.Caption: Reserved Quantity
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how many units of the item on the line have been reserved.

Children.Caption: Unit of Measure Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how each unit of the item or resource is measured, such as in pieces or hours. By default, the value in the Base Unit of Measure field on the item or resource card is inserted.

Children.Caption: Unit Price Excl. VAT
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the price for one unit on the sales line.

Children.Caption: Line Discount %
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the discount percentage that is granted for the item on the line.

Children.Caption: Line Amount Excl. VAT
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the net amount, excluding any invoice discount amount, that must be paid for products on the line.

Children.Caption: Subscription Lines
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of Subscription Lines for the sales line.

Children.Caption: Customer Subscription Contract No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the associated Customer Subscription Contract the Subscription Line will be assigned to. If the sales line was created by a Contract Renewal, the Contract No. cannot be edited.

Children.Caption: Vendor Subscription Contract No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the associated Vendor Subscription Contract the Subscription Line will be assigned to. If the sales line was created by a Contract Renewal, the Contract No. cannot be edited.

Children.Caption: Allocation Account No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the allocation account number that will be used to distribute the amounts during the posting process.

Children.Caption: Qty. to Ship
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the quantity of items that remain to be shipped.

Children.Caption: Quantity Shipped
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how many units of the item on the line have been posted as shipped.

Children.Caption: Qty. to Invoice
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the quantity that remains to be invoiced. It is calculated as Quantity - Qty. Invoiced.

Children.Caption: Quantity Invoiced
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how many units of the item on the line have been posted as invoiced.

Children.Caption: Total CO2e
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the value of the Total CO2e field.

Children.Caption: Total EPR Fee
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the value of the Total EPR Fee field.

Children.Synopsis: Specifies the quantity of the item charge that was assigned to a specified item when you posted this sales line.

Children.Caption: Planned Delivery Date
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the planned date that the shipment will be delivered at the customer's address. If the customer requests a delivery date, the program calculates whether the items will be available for delivery on this date. If the items are available, the planned delivery date will be the same as the requested delivery date. If not, the program calculates the date that the items are available for delivery and enters this date in the Planned Delivery Date field.

Children.Synopsis: Specifies the date that the shipment should ship from the warehouse. If the customer requests a delivery date, the program calculates the planned shipment date by subtracting the shipping time from the requested delivery date. If the customer does not request a delivery date or the requested delivery date cannot be met, the program calculates the content of this field by adding the shipment time to the shipping date.

Children.Caption: Shipment Date
Children.Synopsis: Specifies when items on the document are shipped or were shipped. A shipment date is usually calculated from a requested delivery date plus lead time.

Children.Caption: Afdeling Kode
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 1, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

Children.Caption: Debitorgruppe Kode
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 2, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

Children.Caption: Attached Lines Count
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of non-inventory product lines attached to the sales line.

Children.Name: TotalSalesLine.Line Amount
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the sum of the value in the Line Amount Excl. VAT field on all lines in the document.

Specifies a discount amount that is deducted from the value of the Total Incl. VAT field, based on sales lines where the Allow Invoice Disc. field is selected. You can enter or change the amount manually.

Children.Caption: Invoice Discount %
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a discount percentage that is applied to the invoice, based on sales lines where the Allow Invoice Disc. field is selected. The percentage and criteria are defined in the Customer Invoice Discounts page, but you can enter or change the percentage manually.
Specifies the sum of the value in the Line Amount Excl. VAT field on all lines in the document minus any discount amount in the Invoice Discount Amount field.
Specifies the sum of VAT amounts on all lines in the document.
Specifies the sum of the value in the Line Amount Incl. VAT field on all lines in the document minus any discount amount in the Invoice Discount Amount field.

Children.Caption: New Line
Children.Name: 97614525-6ed6-4ed5-9262-c6e5a8d4e6a5
Children.Synopsis: Create a new entry.

Children.Caption: Delete Line
Children.Name: 7a6412b6-f244-4670-9259-76648c1bb1ef
Children.Synopsis: Delete the selected row

Children.Caption: Select items...
Children.Synopsis: Add two or more items from the full list of your inventory items.

Children.Caption: Open in Excel
Children.Synopsis: Opens an Excel file with the data exported from the list (selected filters and columns).

Children.Caption: Get Price...
Children.Synopsis: Insert the lowest possible price in the Unit Price field according to any special price that you have set up.

Children.Caption: Get Li&ne Discount...
Children.Synopsis: Insert the best possible discount in the Line Discount field according to any special discounts that you have set up.

Children.Caption: Get Price...
Children.Synopsis: Insert the lowest possible price in the Unit Price field according to any special price that you have set up.

Children.Caption: Get Li&ne Discount...
Children.Synopsis: Insert the best possible discount in the Line Discount field according to any special discounts that you have set up.

Children.Caption: E&xplode BOM
Children.Synopsis: Add a line for each component on the bill of materials for the selected item. For example, this is useful for selling the parent item as a kit. CAUTION: The line for the parent item will be deleted and only its description will display. To undo this action, delete the component lines and add a line for the parent item again. This action is available only for lines that contain an item.

Children.Caption: Insert &Ext. Texts
Children.Synopsis: Insert the extended item description that is set up for the item that is being processed on the line.

Children.Caption: Attach to inventory item line
Children.Synopsis: Attach the selected non-inventory product lines to a inventory item line in this sales order.

Children.Caption: &Reserve...
Children.Synopsis: Reserve the quantity of the selected item that is required on the document line from which you opened this page. This action is available only for lines that contain an item.

Children.Caption: Order &Tracking
Children.Synopsis: Track the connection of a supply to its corresponding demand for the selected item. This can help you find the original demand that created a specific production order or purchase order. This action is available only for lines that contain an item.

Children.Caption: Select Ca&talog Items
Children.Synopsis: View the list of catalog items that exist in the system.

Children.Caption: Add Subscription Lines
Children.Synopsis: Shows all Subscription Lines for the item. Subscription Lines can be added, changed or removed.

Children.Caption: Event
Children.Synopsis: View how the actual and the projected available balance of an item will develop over time according to supply and demand events.

Children.Caption: Period
Children.Synopsis: Show the projected quantity of the item over time according to time periods, such as day, week, or month.

Children.Caption: Variant
Children.Synopsis: View or edit the item's variants. Instead of setting up each color of an item as a separate item, you can set up the various colors as variants of the item.

Children.Caption: Location
Children.Synopsis: View the actual and projected quantity of the item per location.

Children.Caption: Lot
Children.Synopsis: View the current and projected quantity of the item for each lot.

Children.Caption: BOM Level
Children.Synopsis: View availability figures for items on bills of materials that show how many units of a parent item you can make based on the availability of child items.

Children.Caption: Unit of Measure
Children.Synopsis: View the item's availability by a unit of measure.

Children.Caption: Reservation Entries
Children.Synopsis: View all reservation entries for the selected item. This action is available only for lines that contain an item.

Children.Caption: Item &Tracking Lines
Children.Synopsis: View or edit serial, lot and package numbers for the selected item. This action is available only for lines that contain an item.

Children.Caption: Select Item Substitution
Children.Synopsis: Select another item that has been set up to be sold instead of the original item if it is unavailable.

Children.Caption: Dimensions
Children.Synopsis: View or edit dimensions, such as area, project, or department, that you can assign to sales and purchase documents to distribute costs and analyze transaction history.

Children.Caption: Co&mments
Children.Synopsis: View or add comments for the record.

Children.Caption: Item Charge &Assignment
Children.Synopsis: Record additional direct costs, for example for freight. This action is available only for Charge (Item) line types.

Children.Caption: Order &Promising
Children.Synopsis: Calculate the shipment and delivery dates based on the item's known and expected availability dates, and then promise the dates to the customer.

Children.Caption: Attachments
Children.Synopsis: Add a file as an attachment. You can attach images as well as documents.

Children.Caption: Assemble-to-Order Lines
Children.Synopsis: View any linked assembly order lines if the documents represents an assemble-to-order sale.

Children.Caption: Roll Up &Price...
Children.Synopsis: Update the unit price of the assembly item according to any changes that you have made to the assembly components.

Children.Caption: Roll Up &Cost...
Children.Synopsis: Update the unit cost of the assembly item according to any changes that you have made to the assembly components.

Children.Caption: Document &Line Tracking
Children.Synopsis: View related open, posted, or archived documents or document lines.

Children.Caption: Deferral Schedule
Children.Synopsis: View or edit the deferral schedule that governs how revenue made with this sales document is deferred to different accounting periods when the document is posted.

Children.Caption: Redistribute Account Allocations
Children.Synopsis: Use this action to redistribute the account allocations for this line.

Children.Caption: Generate lines from Allocation Account Line
Children.Synopsis: Use this action to replace the Allocation Account line with the actual lines that would be generated from the line itself.

Children.Caption: Subscription Lines
Children.Synopsis: Shows the Subscription Lines for the sales line.

Children.Caption: Purchase &Order
Children.Synopsis: View the purchase order that is linked to the sales order, for drop shipment or special order.

Children.Caption: Purchase &Order
Children.Synopsis: View the purchase order that is linked to the sales order, for drop shipment or special order.

Children.Caption: Blanket Order
Children.Synopsis: View the blanket sales order.

Children.Caption: Show Lines with Issues
Children.Synopsis: View a list of sales lines that have issues before you post the document.

Children.Caption: Show All Lines
Children.Synopsis: View all sales lines, including lines with and without issues.

Children.Caption: Edit in Excel
Children.Synopsis: Send the data in the sub page to an Excel file for analysis or editing

Children.Caption: Suggest sales lines
Children.Synopsis: Get sales lines suggestions from Copilot

Children.Caption: Suggest sales lines
Children.Synopsis: Get sales lines suggestions from Copilot

Children.Caption: Suggest sales lines from file...
Children.Synopsis: Get sales lines from file with Copilot

Children.Caption: Currency Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the currency of amounts on the sales document.

Children.Caption: Company Bank Account Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the bank account to use for bank information when the document is printed.

Children.Caption: Prices Including VAT
Children.Synopsis: Specifies if the Unit Price and Line Amount fields on document lines should be shown with or without VAT.

Children.Caption: VAT Country/Region Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the country or region code for the VAT.

Children.Caption: VAT Registration No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the VAT registration number for customers.

Children.Caption: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the general business posting group that the sales document is linked to. The general business posting group is used to link the sales document to the appropriate general ledger account.

Children.Caption: VAT Bus. Posting Group
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the VAT specification of the involved customer or vendor to link transactions made for this record with the appropriate general ledger account according to the VAT posting setup.

Children.Caption: Customer Posting Group
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's market type to link business transactions to.

Children.Caption: Payment Terms Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a formula that calculates the payment due date, payment discount date, and payment discount amount.

Children.Caption: Payment Method Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how to make payment, such as with bank transfer, cash, or check.

Children.Caption: EU 3-Party Trade
Children.Synopsis: Specifies if the transaction is related to trade with a third party within the EU.

Children.Caption: Payment Service
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the online payment service, such as PayPal, that customers can use to pay the sales document.

Children.Caption: Afdeling Kode
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 1, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

Children.Caption: Debitorgruppe Kode
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 2, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

Children.Caption: Payment Discount %
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the payment discount percentage granted if the customer pays on or before the date entered in the Pmt. Discount Date field.

Children.Caption: Pmt. Discount Date
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the date on which the amount in the entry must be paid for a payment discount to be granted.

Children.Caption: Journal Template Name
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the name of the journal template in which the sales header is to be posted.

Children.Caption: Direct Debit Mandate ID
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the direct-debit mandate that the customer has signed to allow direct debit collection of payments.

Children.Caption: Ship-to
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the address that the products on the sales document are shipped to. Default (Sell-to Address): The same as the customer's sell-to address. Alternate Ship-to Address: One of the customer's alternate ship-to addresses. Custom Address: Any ship-to address that you specify in the fields below.

Children.Caption: Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the code for another shipment address than the customer's own address, which is entered by default.

Children.Caption: Name
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the name that products on the sales document will be shipped to.

Children.Caption: Address
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the address that products on the sales document will be shipped to.

Children.Caption: Address 2
Children.Synopsis: Specifies additional address information.

Children.Caption: City
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the city of the customer on the sales document.

Children.Caption: Ship-to County
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the state, province or county of the address.

Children.Caption: Post Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the postal code.

Children.Caption: Country/Region
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's country/region.

Children.Caption: Phone No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the telephone number of the company's shipping address.

Children.Caption: Contact
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the name of the contact person at the address that products on the sales document will be shipped to.

Children.Caption: Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how items on the sales document are shipped to the customer.

Children.Caption: Agent
Children.Synopsis: Specifies which shipping agent is used to transport the items on the sales document to the customer.

Children.Caption: Agent Service
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the code that represents the default shipping agent service you are using for this sales order.

Children.Caption: Package Tracking No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the shipping agent's package number.

Children.Caption: Bill-to
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer that the sales invoice will be sent to. Default (Customer): The same as the customer on the sales invoice. Another Customer: Any customer that you specify in the fields below.

Children.Caption: GLN
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the GLN location number for the customer. This is used in the exported electronic document.

Children.Caption: Account Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the account code of the customer. This is used in the exported electronic document.

Children.Caption: Profile Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the profile that the customer requires for electronic documents. This is used in the exported electronic document.

Children.Caption: Name
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer to whom you will send the sales invoice, when different from the customer that you are selling to.

Children.Caption: Address
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the address of the customer that you will send the invoice to.

Children.Caption: Address 2
Children.Synopsis: Specifies additional address information.

Children.Caption: City
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the city of the customer on the sales document.

Children.Caption: Bill-to County
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the state, province or county of the address.

Children.Caption: Post Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the postal code.

Children.Caption: Country/Region Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the country or region of the address.

Children.Caption: Contact No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of the contact the invoice will be sent to.

Children.Caption: Contact
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the name of the person you should contact at the customer who you are sending the invoice to.

Children.Caption: Phone No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the telephone number of the person you should contact at the customer you are sending the invoice to.

Children.Caption: Mobile Phone No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the mobile telephone number of the person you should contact at the customer you are sending the invoice to.

Children.Caption: Email
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the email address of the person you should contact at the customer you are sending the invoice to.

Children.Caption: Location Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the location from where items are to be shipped. This field acts as the default location for new lines. You can update the location code for individual lines as needed.

Children.Caption: Shipment Date
Children.Synopsis: Specifies when items on the document are shipped or were shipped. A shipment date is usually calculated from a requested delivery date plus lead time.

Children.Caption: Shipping Advice
Children.Synopsis: Specifies if the customer accepts partial shipment of orders.

Children.Caption: Outbound Whse. Handling Time
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a date formula for the time it takes to get items ready to ship from this location. The time element is used in the calculation of the delivery date as follows: Shipment Date + Outbound Warehouse Handling Time = Planned Shipment Date + Shipping Time = Planned Delivery Date.

Children.Caption: Shipping Time
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how long it takes from when the items are shipped from the warehouse to when they are delivered.

Children.Caption: Late Order Shipping
Children.Synopsis: Indicates a delay in the shipment of one or more lines, or that the shipment date is either the same as or earlier than the work date.

Children.Caption: Combine Shipments
Children.Synopsis: Specifies whether the order will be included when you use the Combine Shipments function.

Children.Caption: Completely Shipped
Children.Synopsis: Specifies whether all the items on the order have been shipped or, in the case of inbound items, completely received.

Children.Caption: Transaction Type
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the type of transaction that the document represents, for the purpose of reporting to INTRASTAT.

Children.Caption: Prepayment %
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the prepayment percentage to use to calculate the prepayment for sales.

Children.Caption: Compress Prepayment
Children.Synopsis: Specifies that prepayments on the sales order are combined if they have the same general ledger account for prepayments or the same dimensions.

Children.Caption: Prepmt. Payment Terms Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the code that represents the payment terms for prepayment invoices related to the sales document.

Children.Caption: Prepayment Due Date
Children.Synopsis: Specifies when the prepayment invoice for this sales order is due.

Children.Caption: Prepmt. Payment Discount %
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the payment discount percent granted on the prepayment if the customer pays on or before the date entered in the Prepmt. Pmt. Discount Date field.

Children.Caption: Prepmt. Pmt. Discount Date
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the last date the customer can pay the prepayment invoice and still receive a payment discount on the prepayment amount.

FilterColumns.Caption: Amount Shipped Not Invoiced (LCY) Incl. VAT

Children.Caption: Issues Total
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of issues that have been found in the document.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the number of issues that have been found in the document.

Children.Caption: Refresh
Children.Synopsis: Refresh the document check.

Children.Caption: Open in Excel
Children.Synopsis: Opens an Excel file with the data exported from the list (selected filters and columns).

Columns.Caption: Name
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the name of the attached file.

Columns.Caption: File Extension
Columns.Synopsis: Specifies the file extension of the attachment.

Children.Synopsis: Specifies the name of the attached file.

Children.Caption: File Extension
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the file extension of the attachment.

Children.Synopsis: Copy the file to your Business Central folder in OneDrive and open it in a new window so you can manage or share the file.

Children.Synopsis: Copy the file to your Business Central folder in OneDrive and open it in a new window so you can edit the file.

Children.Synopsis: Copy the file to your Business Central folder in OneDrive and share the file. You can also see who it's already shared with.

Children.Synopsis: Download the file to your device. Depending on the file, you will need an app to view or edit the file.

Children.Synopsis: Attach files directly from email.

Children.Synopsis: Copy the file to your Business Central folder in OneDrive and open it in a new window so you can manage or share the file.

Children.Synopsis: Copy the file to your Business Central folder in OneDrive and open it in a new window so you can edit the file.

Children.Caption: Sender ID
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the ID of the user who sent the approval request for the document to be approved.

Children.Caption: Approval Due Date
Children.Synopsis: Specifies when the record must be approved, by one or more approvers.

Children.Caption: Comment
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a comment that applies to the approval entry.

Children.Caption: Customer No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of the customer. The field is either filled automatically from a defined number series, or you enter the number manually because you have enabled manual number entry in the number-series setup.

Children.Caption: Ongoing Sales Quotes
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of sales quotes that have been registered for the customer.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the number of sales quotes that have been registered for the customer.

Children.Caption: Ongoing Sales Blanket Orders
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of sales blanket orders that have been registered for the customer.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the number of sales blanket orders that have been registered for the customer.

Children.Caption: Ongoing Sales Orders
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of sales orders that have been registered for the customer.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the number of sales orders that have been registered for the customer.

Children.Caption: Ongoing Sales Invoices
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of unposted sales invoices that have been registered for the customer.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the number of unposted sales invoices that have been registered for the customer.

Children.Caption: Ongoing Sales Return Orders
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of sales return orders that have been registered for the customer.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the number of sales return orders that have been registered for the customer.

Children.Caption: Ongoing Sales Credit Memos
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of unposted sales credit memos that have been registered for the customer.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the number of unposted sales credit memos that have been registered for the customer.

Children.Caption: Posted Sales Shipments
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of posted sales shipments that have been registered for the customer.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the number of posted sales shipments that have been registered for the customer.

Children.Caption: Posted Sales Invoices
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of posted sales invoices that have been registered for the customer.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the number of posted sales invoices that have been registered for the customer.

Children.Caption: Posted Sales Return Receipts
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of posted sales return receipts that have been registered for the customer.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the number of posted sales return receipts that have been registered for the customer.

Children.Caption: Posted Sales Credit Memos
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of posted sales credit memos that have been registered for the customer.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the number of posted sales credit memos that have been registered for the customer.

Children.Caption: Customer Subscription Contracts
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of Customer Subscription Contracts that have been registered for the customer.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the number of Customer Subscription Contracts that have been registered for the customer.

Children.Caption: Subscriptions
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of Subscriptions that have been registered for the customer.
Children.CustomTooltipText: Specifies the number of Subscriptions that have been registered for the customer.

Children.Caption: Customer No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of the customer. The field is either filled automatically from a defined number series, or you enter the number manually because you have enabled manual number entry in the number-series setup.

Children.Caption: Name
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's name that appears on all related documents. For companies, specify the company's name here, and then add the relevant people as contacts that you link to this customer.

Children.Caption: Phone No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's telephone number.

Children.Caption: Email
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's email address.

Children.Caption: Fax No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the customer's fax number.

Children.Caption: Credit Limit (LCY)
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the maximum amount of credit that you extend to the customer for their purchases before you issue warnings. The value 0 represents unlimited credit.

Children.Caption: Available Credit (LCY)
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a customer's available credit. If the available credit is 0 and the customer's credit limit is also 0, then the customer has unlimited credit because no credit limit has been defined.

Children.Caption: Payment Terms Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies a code that indicates the payment terms that you require of the customer.

Children.Caption: Contact
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the name of the person you regularly contact when you do business with this customer.

Children.Caption: Ship-to Address
Children.Synopsis: View the ship-to address that is specified for the customer.

Children.Caption: Comments
Children.Synopsis: View or add comments for the record.

Children.Caption: Item No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the item that is handled on the sales line.

Children.Caption: Required Quantity
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how many units of the item are required on the sales line.

Children.Caption: Subscription Lines
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of Subscription Lines for the sales line.

Children.Caption: Documents
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of attachments.

Children.Caption: Shipment Date
Children.Synopsis: Specifies when the items on the sales line must be shipped.

Children.Caption: Item Availability
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how may units of the item on the sales line are available, in inventory or incoming before the shipment date.

Children.Caption: Available Inventory
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the quantity of the item that is currently in inventory and not reserved for other demand.

Children.Caption: Scheduled Receipt
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how many units of the assembly component are inbound on purchase orders, transfer orders, assembly orders, firm planned production orders, and released production orders.

Children.Caption: Reserved Receipt
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how many units of the item on the sales line are reserved on incoming receipts.

Children.Caption: Reserved Requirements
Children.Synopsis: Specifies, for the item on the sales line, how many are reserved on demand records.

Children.Caption: Reserved from stock
Children.Synopsis: Specifies what part of the sales line is reserved from inventory.

Children.Caption: Unit of Measure Code
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the unit of measure that is used to determine the value in the Unit Price field on the sales line.

Children.Caption: Qty. per Unit of Measure
Children.Synopsis: Specifies an auto-filled number if you have included Sales Unit of Measure on the item card and a quantity in the Qty. per Unit of Measure field.

Children.Caption: Substitutions
Children.Synopsis: Specifies other items that are set up to be traded instead of the item in case it is not available.

Children.Caption: Sales Prices
Children.Synopsis: Specifies special sales prices that you grant when certain conditions are met, such as customer, quantity, or ending date. The price agreements can be for individual customers, for a group of customers, for all customers or for a campaign.

Children.Caption: Sales Line Discounts
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how many special discounts you grant for the sales line. Choose the value to see the sales line discounts.

Children.Caption: Item No.
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the number of the item.

Children.Caption: Costing Method
Children.Synopsis: Specifies how the item's cost flow is recorded and whether an actual or budgeted value is capitalized and used in the cost calculation.

Children.Caption: Cost is Adjusted
Children.Synopsis: Specifies whether the item's unit cost has been adjusted, either automatically or manually.

Children.Caption: Cost is Posted to G/L
Children.Synopsis: Specifies that all the inventory costs for this item have been posted to the general ledger.

Children.Caption: Standard Cost
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the unit cost that is used as an estimation to be adjusted with variances later. It is typically used in assembly and production where costs can vary.

Children.Caption: Unit Cost
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the cost of one unit of the item or resource on the line.

Children.Caption: Overhead Rate
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the item's indirect cost as an absolute amount.

Children.Caption: Indirect Cost %
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the percentage of the item's last purchase cost that includes indirect costs, such as freight that is associated with the purchase of the item.

Children.Caption: Last Direct Cost
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the most recent direct unit cost of the item.

Children.Caption: Profit %
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the profit margin that you want to sell the item at. You can enter a profit percentage manually or have it entered according to the Price/Profit Calculation field

Children.Caption: Unit Price
Children.Synopsis: Specifies the price of one unit of the item or resource. You can enter a price manually or have it entered according to the Price/Profit Calculation field on the related card.
